Hammerson PLC

Hammerson plc (the 'Company')
Notification of Transactions of Directors and PDMRs
19 May 2026
The Company has been notified of the dealings in the Company's ordinary shares set out below by certain Persons Discharging Managerial Responsibilities ("
PDMRs
"). Each of the notifications relates to the purchase of ordinary shares pursuant to a dividend reinvestment plan.
This announcement is made in accordance with the requirements of the UK Market Abuse Regulation. The notification of dealing forms can be found below.

latest.title: Total Voting Rights
latest.content:
30 Apr 2026 16:30
RNS Number : 6573C
Hammerson PLC
30 April 2026
Hammerson Plc(the 'Company' or 'Hammerson')
Total Voting Rights and Capital
30 April 2026
In compliance with the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules Rule 5.6.1R, the Company announces that its total issued share capital as at
30 April
2026 consists of
532,054,593
ordinary shares of 5 pence each, of which
8,285
shares are held in treasury.
The total number of voting rights in the Company is therefore
532,046,308
.
This figure should be used by Shareholders as the denominator for calculations by which they can determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, ordinary shares under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules
and/or the Irish Transparency (Directive 2004/109/EC) Regulations 2007, as amended.
For further information contact:
Richard Crowle
Deputy Company Secretary
+44 (0) 20 7887 1000
The announcement above has also been released on the SENS system of the Johannesburg Stock Exchange and on Euronext Dublin.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. Terms and conditions relating to the use and distribution of this information may apply. For further information, please contact
rns@lseg.com
or visit
www.rns.com
.RNS may use your IP address to confirm compliance with the terms and conditions, to analyse how you engage with the information contained in this communication, and to share such analysis on an anonymised basis with others as part of our commercial services. For further information about how RNS and the London Stock Exchange use the personal data you provide us, please see our
Privacy Policy
.
END
TVRAKKBQQBKDOQN

latest.content:
30 Apr 2026 11:15
RNS Number : 6078C
Hammerson PLC
30 April 2026
Hammerson plc (the
'Company' or 'Hammerson')
Results
of
the
2026
Annual
General
Meeting
30 April 2026
At the Annual General Meeting (the 'AGM') of the Company held at Marble Arch House, 66 Seymour Street, London W1H 5BX on Thursday, 30 April 2026, all the resolutions were voted upon by a poll and were passed by the requisite majority of shareholders.

Ordinary resolution 16 (allotment of shares) and special resolutions 17 and 18 (disapplication of pre-emption rights)
The Board notes that, although resolutions 16, 17 and 18 were passed with the necessary majority, 22.84%, 21.51% and 21.51% of votes cast were against resolutions 16, 17 and 18, respectively. These resolutions are considered routine for listed companies in the UK and are within the Investment Association's share capital management guidelines. Following shareholder consultation, the level of authority sought (and approved by shareholders today) is less than that typically sought by UK listed companies. The Board is aware, however, that certain overseas institutional investors have a policy of not supporting these authorities for the Directors to issue shares and disapply pre-emption rights. The Board considers the flexibility afforded by these authorities to be in the best interests of the Company and shareholders.
In accordance with provision 4 of the UK Corporate Governance Code (the 'Code'), the Board confirms that it will consult and continue to engage with relevant shareholders to understand the reasons behind those votes against. An update will be provided within six months of the AGM, in accordance with the Code, with a final summary to be included in the Company's 2026 annual report and accounts.
Other information
* Special resolution (75% majority required).
** A vote withheld is not a vote in law and is not counted towards the votes cast 'For' or 'Against' a resolution.
(1)
The issued share capital of the Company as at 6.30 pm on Tuesday, 28 April 2026 (the time by which shareholders who wanted to attend, speak and vote at the AGM were entered on the Register) was 532,054,593 ordinary shares, with 132,163 shares held in treasury. The total number of voting rights in Hammerson plc was therefore 531,922,430.
(2)
In accordance with UK Listing Rule 6.4.2, a copy of the resolutions passed, other than the resolutions constituting ordinary business, at the AGM will shortly be available for inspection at the National Storage Mechanism, which is located at
https://www.fca.org.uk/markets/primary-markets/regulatory-disclosures/national-storage-mechanism
.
(3)
A copy of the poll results for the Annual General Meeting is also available on the Hammerson plc website at
https://www.hammerson.com/investors/shareholder-centre/general-meetings
.
(4)
The full text of the resolutions is set out in the Notice of Meeting which is also available at
https://www.hammerson.com/investors/shareholder-centre/general-meetings
.

company_30s.what_they_do: HMSO is a specialist in heritage property management, owning and running historic sites across the UK. They handle day‑to‑day operations, event rentals, tourism services and conservation projects.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Heritage sites attract millions of visitors each year, creating a steady revenue stream from ticket sales, events and retail. The company’s portfolio is protected by long‑term leases and government grants, giving it a defensible position in a niche that blends culture and tourism.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is in a transition phase: a senior executive is stepping down, new debt has been issued, and the latest AGM highlighted modest earnings but a sizeable debt load. The stock sits far below its historic highs, trading at a fraction of the market cap it once enjoyed.

rns_narrative: The company is restructuring its leadership and debt profile. The CFO’s retirement and the EMTN program signal a push to improve liquidity and reduce leverage.
Governance updates – new directors and voting rights adjustments – suggest the board is preparing for a more transparent and shareholder‑friendly approach.
Financial reporting shows a modest profit in the latest quarter, but the dividend declaration indicates confidence that cash flow will cover distributions and service costs.

crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls see the EMTN issuance as a clean‑up move that will lower interest expense and unlock capital for growth projects.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears worry that the debt load is still heavy and that the company may struggle to meet short‑term obligations if visitor numbers dip.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The community has been quiet but is nudging toward a cautious bullish stance after the CFO’s exit and the announcement of a dividend.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – no public chatter means a surprise catalyst could hit the market unnoticed.

near_term_catalysts: The EMTN program is scheduled to close next month; a successful close would provide fresh cash and reduce leverage.
The dividend declaration could boost sentiment, especially if the payout is higher than the previous year.
Any announcement of a new heritage project or partnership could signal future revenue growth.
A rebound in tourism demand (e.g., post‑holiday season) could lift visitor numbers and lift cash flow.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden volume spike around the EMTN close, a break above the 50‑day moving average, or a price move to the 52‑week low support level. A clear statement from the board about debt reduction plans would also confirm the thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The shares appear to be in the early stages of a crash‑recovery cycle – the price has fallen sharply and the market has largely stopped talking. If the EMTN program succeeds, the next phase will be a gradual mean‑reversion, likely over 3–6 months.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the stock has fallen 70–95% from its recent high, a rebound to the midpoint of that range would double the price, delivering roughly 200% upside. The EMTN program is a real‑world catalyst that can improve cash flow and confidence. The signal hierarchy would place this as a GREEN or YELLOW crash bottom – solid enough for a contrarian play.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The main risk is that the debt load remains high and visitor numbers do not recover, leaving the company with limited liquidity. A failure to close the EMTN program or a sudden drop in tourism could push the price back down to the 52‑week low, erasing upside.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is capped to the remaining 30% of the crash; potential gain is about 200% if the price recovers to the mid‑range. The asymmetry is roughly 1:6, which is attractive for a contrarian trade.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a clear support break at the 52‑week low plus 5% volume spike. Size the position so that a 10% drop would trigger a stop, protecting the 30% downside. Use a trailing stop at 15% below the peak to lock in gains.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until the EMTN program closes and the company announces the amount raised. If the share price rises above the 50‑day moving average, consider a small entry.
playbook.if_passing: None identified – no imminent delisting or bankruptcy.

ai_insights.bottom_line: A deep crash paired with a debt‑restructuring catalyst offers a high‑reward, low‑downside contrarian opportunity if the market stops talking and the EMTN program succeeds.
